description: John Zenon, a 32-year-old structural welder, presents to the occupational health clinic with severe bilateral eye pain, photophobia, tearing, and a 'sand in the eyes' sensation following an accidental exposure to intense ultraviolet radiation due to a malfunctioning welding helmet. Clinical examination reveals diffuse punctate corneal abrasions consistent with ultraviolet keratitis (welder's arc eye). As the occupational health physician, complete his acute management protocol in GNU Health: 1. Document the photokeratitis diagnosis as an active condition (ICD-10 H16.x or T26.x). 2. Record a clinical evaluation for today. 3. Prescribe an oral NSAID (e.g., Ibuprofen, Diclofenac, Naproxen). 4. Prescribe a topical ophthalmic antibiotic (e.g., Erythromycin, Tetracycline, Ciprofloxacin) to prevent secondary infection. 5. Schedule a follow-up appointment for John Zenon exactly 1 or 2 days from today. Login: admin / gnusolidario.
